title: Making bokashi bran at home
subtitle: Inoculating wheat bran with EM1 solution to produce a year's supply of bokashi activator for around 15 pounds
excerpt: Commercial bokashi bran costs 8 to 12 pounds for a 1 kg bag, which lasts a single-household system about 6 weeks. Making your own from wheat bran and EM1 solution costs around 15 pounds for a 5 kg batch that lasts 6 to 8 months. The process takes an hour of active work and 10 to 14 days of maturing time.
sourceNotes: Effective Microorganisms Research Organisation (EMRO) technical guidance on EM1 production. Dr Teruo Higa original research publications on EM applications. Bokashi Living guidance on making activated EM (AEM) and bokashi bran. GreenAlly guide to DIY bokashi bran.

glossaryTerms.definition: A commercial concentrate of effective microorganisms in a dormant state. Diluted in water with molasses (a food for the microorganisms) to create an activated EM (AEM) solution that is then mixed with bran and allowed to ferment.

Wheat bran inoculated with effective microorganisms and partially fermented. The dried bran acts as a concentrated source of EM that is sprinkled over food waste in a bokashi bin to initiate fermentation.

content.text: is simply wheat bran inoculated with

and fermented. You need three ingredients: wheat bran (1 to 2 pounds per kg from farm shops or bulk food stores), EM1 concentrate (8 to 12 pounds per 500 ml online), and unsulphured blackstrap molasses (around 3 pounds per 500 g).
Mix 15 ml of EM1 with 15 ml of unsulphured molasses in 500 ml of non-chlorinated water (filtered or left overnight). This is the activated EM (AEM) solution.
Weigh out 1 kg of wheat bran into a large bowl or bucket.
Pour the AEM solution over the bran and mix thoroughly. The bran should be uniformly damp but not waterlogged. Test: squeeze a handful; it should hold shape but not drip water.
Pack tightly into an airtight bag or container with no air pockets. Press out all air.
Leave at room temperature (18 to 25 degrees Celsius) for 10 to 14 days. Do not open the container during this period. The bran will smell sour-sweet (lactic acid) when ready.
Optional: spread on trays to dry at room temperature for 2 to 3 days, then store in a paper bag or open container. Dried bran keeps for 6 months. Undried bran keeps for 4 weeks in the fridge.

A 5 kg batch uses 5x the recipe: 75 ml EM1, 75 ml molasses, 2.5 litres of water, 5 kg bran. The cost is around 13 to 16 pounds for 5 kg of finished bran, versus 50 to 60 pounds for the same weight bought ready-made.

Putrid smell indicates the bran was too wet, the container had air gaps, or the EM1 was out of date (check the expiry date). Discard and restart. Check the EM1 bottle: viable EM1 is slightly acidic (pH 3.5 to 4.5). EM1 that smells putrid or shows a pH above 5 is no longer viable.
